title: Vegetable Pakoda (Restaurant Style)

ingredients.title: For the batter:
items: 1 cup chickpea flour (besan) — sifted for smooth texture
1 tsp ajwain seeds (carom seeds) — helps digestion, adds aroma
½–1 tsp red chili powder (to taste)

ingredients.title: Vegetables:
items: ½ cup carrot — julienned or grated
½ cup cauliflower florets — small bite-sized pieces
1 tbsp fresh spinach — optional, chopped for color variation

ingredients.title: For frying:
items: Neutral oil (mustard oil for traditional flavor or sunflower/peanut oil) — enough for deep frying

instruction.title: Prep the vegetables
steps: Slice onion thin, shred cabbage, julienne carrots, and break cauliflower into small florets. Pat dry if watery — helps crispness.

instruction.title: Make the batter
steps: In a large mixing bowl, combine chickpea flour, rice flour, ajwain seeds, turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, and salt.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, and coriander leaves. Mix dry.

instruction.title: Combine vegetables with batter
steps: Add all vegetables to the dry mix. Toss so they’re coated in the spices before adding water — this makes seasoning even.
Sprinkle water a little at a time until you have a thick, clinging batter — not runny. The vegetables should be just bound together, not swimming in liquid.

instruction.title: Heat the oil
steps: Heat oil in a deep, heavy pan or kadai to 170–180 °C (340–355 °F). Test by dropping a little batter — it should sizzle and rise without burning.

instruction.title: Fry in small batches
steps: Take small handfuls of the mixture and drop gently into the oil. Avoid perfect balls — irregular edges give more crunch.
Fry until golden brown, about 3–4 minutes, turning occasionally.

instruction.title: Drain & serve
steps: Remove pakodas with a slotted spoon, drain on paper towels. Sprinkle a pinch of chaat masala for extra zing.
Serve hot with green chutney or tamarind chutney.
Pair with hot masala chai for a street-food vibe.

chefTips: Serve hot with green chutney or tamarind chutney.
Pair with hot masala chai for a street-food vibe.
